name: Fullington Auto Bus Co
owner_or_parent: independent (Fullington family; re-purchased 100% from RATP Dev in Dec 2017)
recent_news: Dec 2017: Michael & Lory Fullington re-purchased 100% of stock from RATP Dev, returning the company to single-Fullington family ownership for the first time since 1918; ~220 school buses across 6 districts (~30% of mix).
fit_notes: Strong PA platform-scale target (~220 school buses across 6 districts) in Summit's core PA geography. 3rd-gen family-owned; sole owner Mike Fullington previously sold majority to RATP Dev then bought back — has clear precedent for accepting outside capital. Mixed-model (school + motorcoach) may require carve-out.

name: Student Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Likely affiliated with Scholastic Bus Co (Fair Lawn, NJ) - acquired by Student Transportation of America (STA) July 2013; STA is owned by Caisse de depot et placement du Quebec (CDPQ) since 2018
principals: John W. Ely (listed CEO in source data; historically VP at Scholastic Bus)
Michael Ely (President of Scholastic Bus historically)
districts_or_customers: NJ and NY school districts (~30 customers, ~20,000 students daily per legacy Scholastic figures)
recent_news: Scholastic Bus Co (the Ely family operating entity at Fair Lawn HQ) was acquired by Student Transportation of America in July 2013; STA itself was taken private by CDPQ in 2018. The 'Student Bus Co' name and Fair Lawn address (PO Box 1130) correspond to NJSBCA member listings tied to this group.
website: unconfirmed (source listed nysbca.com — that is the NY School Bus Contractors Assoc, not company-owned)
fit_notes: Likely already inside STA/CDPQ platform — not an independent tuck-in target. Worth confirming whether 'Student Bus Co' is a separate Ely family residual entity (small charter/spot-market vehicle) vs. legacy name still under STA. If genuinely independent residual, NJ/NY metro footprint would be highly strategic; if STA-owned, deprioritize.

name: Fullington School Bus
owner_or_parent: Subsidiary/division of Fullington Auto Bus Co (Mike & Lory Fullington)
recent_news: Operates as the Clearfield-HQ school bus arm of Fullington Auto Bus Co; same 2017 100%-family-ownership reclamation applies (Mike & Lory Fullington bought back from RATP Dev).
fit_notes: Same Fullington family enterprise as rank #1 (likely duplicate/sister LLC at Clearfield HQ). Any approach should be consolidated with #1 — single conversation with Mike Fullington. Long-tenured rural Central PA contracts are attractive sticky revenue.

name: Care-A-Van
owner_or_parent: independent (per source data, Craig Mackin listed as Owner)
districts_or_customers: unconfirmed — likely Treasure Coast / St. Lucie County medical and disability transport; possibly contracts with Council on Aging of St. Lucie or paratransit subcontracts
recent_news: No public M&A, news, or press coverage located. Web presence at careavan.com returned an expired SSL certificate, suggesting low digital activity / very small operator. No school district contracts found.
website: careavan.com (SSL expired — may be defunct or dormant)
fit_notes: Sub-scale NEMT/paratransit operator (~$2.5M, 43 employees) with no confirmed school contracts — outside Summit's core school-bus thesis. Only relevant if Summit pursues NEMT/special-needs adjacency in FL. Expired website + 1964 founding suggests possible succession opportunity but very small bolt-on.

name: First Class Transportation Inc
owner_or_parent: independent per source data (Lorraine Wood listed as Owner)
districts_or_customers: unconfirmed — Pasco County / Tampa Bay area NEMT; no school district contracts identified
recent_news: No M&A activity, press, or notable news found. Note: firstclasstransinc.com domain actually resolves to an Atlanta-based family-owned NEMT operator founded 2001, NOT the Hudson, FL entity in the source — the source's website attribution appears incorrect. Two unrelated FMCSA carriers under similar names exist in OK and IN, neither matching Hudson FL.
website: unconfirmed (source's firstclasstransinc.com appears to be a different Atlanta GA entity)
fit_notes: Very small (~13 employees, $1.2M rev) likely owner-operator NEMT — too small to be a meaningful Summit tuck-in and outside core school-bus thesis. Listed website appears mis-attributed in source data. Deprioritize unless Summit is rolling up small FL NEMT shops.
